metric_name: HP stock decline on Capellas resignation
metric_value: HPQ fell 11% on day of Capellas resignation announcement
notes: Date: November 15, 2002; specific market data cited in study opening

metric_value: Capellas departure not worth 11% hit on HP long-term prospects — selloff is overreaction
notes: Core analytical claim of the InSight

metric_name: HP strategic positioning at merger +14 months
metric_value: More bullish than at merger announcement 14 months prior despite disappointing near-term quarters
notes: Kastner's overall assessment after 14 months of post-merger observation

metric_value: StorageWorks identified as key strategic pillar supporting long-term HP competitiveness
notes: Storage explicitly listed in keyword metadata as strategic focus

metric_value: ZLE framework positions HP as real-time enterprise infrastructure provider
notes: ZLE concept co-developed by Aberdeen and HP; never achieved independent industry recognition

metric_value: Itanium identified as key strategic pillar for HP enterprise server differentiation
notes: Itanium was central to HP server roadmap in 2002; ultimately a commercial failure

metric_value: Bluestone J2EE middleware identified as HP enterprise software platform strength
notes: Bluestone acquired by HP 2001; ultimately discontinued as Java middleware market matured

metric_value: OpenView identified as HP enterprise management platform strength
notes: OpenView was a genuine market leader in IT management; eventually succeeded by other tools

metric_value: iPaq (from Compaq) identified as HP mobile device strength in enterprise mobility
notes: iPaq was market-leading PDA in 2002; displaced by smartphones post-2007

metric_value: Dell identified as primary PC market competitor; HP scale from Compaq merger creates competitive advantage
notes: Dell keyword in study metadata confirms competitive focus

metric_name: Sun Microsystems competitive threat to HP
metric_value: Sun identified as enterprise server competitor; HP/Compaq merger creates scale to challenge Sun
notes: Sun keyword in study metadata; Sun ultimately acquired by Oracle 2010

metric_value: HP trajectory will prove more positive than market perceives at Capellas departure; execution is key
notes: Core thesis of InSight — bullish on HP despite near-term headwinds

metric_value: Itanium positioned as strategic enterprise server platform differentiator for HP
notes: Implicit prediction that Itanium would succeed commercially

metric_value: Capellas became CEO of WorldCom/MCI Nov 2002 (same week); led MCI through bankruptcy and Verizon merger (2006)
notes: Capellas' departure immediately explained — went to WorldCom

metric_value: HP survived; Carly Fiorina fired Feb 2005; Mark Hurd led recovery; HP split into HPE + HP Inc. Nov 2015
notes: Kastner's bullish thesis partially confirmed — HP survived; mixed execution results

metric_value: Itanium commercial failure; Intel ended production 2019; last HP systems shipped 2021; x86-64 dominated market
notes: Prediction OBS-013 failed; Itanium was one of history's most expensive commercial chip failures
